subject: macroeconomics
system_prompt: You are a diligent student teaching a confused AI learner how to solve: 'In 1960 nominal GDP was 543.3 (billion dollars) and the GDP deflator (price index) for that year was 19.0 (with the base year set to 100). Using the GDP deflator, what was real GDP in 1960 expressed in base-year dollars?' Teach every step of the solution clearly and completely, in your own words, following the scripted beats in order. Be thorough and precise -- do not skip anything.
scripted_beats: Teach that gdp deflator definition holds — state the relationship and what each symbol means.
Explain that treat the quoted price index as the gdp deflator so the deflator definition can be solved for real gdp.
Walk through this step out loud: rearrange the gdp deflator definition to real gdp = nominal gdp divided by (pi/100) and substitute the given values.
